notes: db: neo4j-driver is used directly (raw Cypher via driver.session().run(...)) in src/app/api/auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts and src/app/api/families/settings/route.ts — owned db access, not a hosted-API consumer. distributed: next-auth's EmailProvider is configured in src/app/api/auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts with an SMTP transport (host/port/auth/from) — this is next-auth's built-in nodemailer-backed transport (nodemailer is a direct package.json dependency and next-auth's EmailProvider requires it at runtime to send magic-link email), a side-effectful remote boundary (sends real email) even though the file has no literal `import nodemailer`. No separate file imports nodemailer directly — grepped the whole web/ tree, found none — so route.ts stands as the sole representative source for both neo4j-driver and the nodemailer-backed distributed boundary; not fabricated, this is genuinely how the repo wires it. react: .tsx components present throughout (SignInForm.tsx uses react-hook-form + next-auth/react client SDK). No test files exist in the repo (grepped for *.test.*/*.spec.*, zero matches).

rules_notes.dependency-at-the-edges: + src/app/api/families/settings/route.ts — new Date() in business code and raw process.env.NEO4J_* at the driver call site, outside a composition root
rules_notes.integration-point-isolation: + src/app/api/families/settings/route.ts — neo4j.driver(...) instantiated here AND in the nextauth route — same client from two modules
rules_notes.external-call-timeout: + src/app/api/families/settings/route.ts — driver.session().run(...) network calls carry no timeout/deadline
rules_notes.remote-call-has-a-third-outcome: + src/app/api/families/settings/route.ts — await session.run returns FamilySettings | null — no distinct timed-out/no-response arm
rules_notes.parse-dont-validate-at-every-untyped-bou: + src/app/api/families/settings/route.ts — JSON.parse(settingsNode.familyValues) and request.json() feed typed FamilySettings with no parse step
rules_notes.external-contract-not-internal-schema: + src/app/api/families/settings/route.ts — handlers NextResponse.json(settings) serialize the internal persistence type straight to the HTTP boundary
rules_notes.mood-names-commands-facts: - no event bus, message queue, or command/event tag vocabulary anywhere
rules_notes.stale-message-is-dropped-by-version: - no subscription/stream/socket handler writing received state
rules_notes.no-shared-mutable-state-across-workers: - no worker/job execution model; no cross-worker shared mutable binding
rules_notes.per-unit-crash-isolation: - no batch/loop over independent units — only per-request route handlers
rules_notes.idempotent-handler-by-dedup-key: - no redelivery-capable delivery layer; writes are synchronous request-scoped handlers
rules_notes.restart-policy-as-data: - no external queue/supervisor, no retry-counter/backoff logic
rules_notes.accountants-dont-use-erasers: - only mutable User/FamilySettings nodes; no ledger/journal/append-only fact store
